summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the CreateNewDAG API endpoint accepts a name field containing path separators and writes attacker-controlled content to an arbitrary filesystem path outside the DAG store directory, without any authentication when auth.mode is set to none.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the crafted DAG creation request to write a file containing a unique proof marker to /tmp/evohunt-proof-<timestamp>, which is outside the configured DAG directory. The verification script confirms the file exists outside the DAG directory and contains the expected marker.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the target repository, built the dagu binary from source, started the server with auth.mode=none, sent a POST /api/v1/dags request with a name containing /tmp/evohunt-proof-<timestamp>, and the verification script confirmed the file was created at the specified path outside the DAG directory with the expected proof marker content.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks three conditions: (1) the proof file exists at the attacker-specified path outside the DAG directory, (2) the proof file contains the unique marker string EVOHUNT-PATH-TRAVERSAL-PROOF, and (3) the file's parent directory is different from the configured DAG directory, confirming the path traversal. A failure in any check causes the script to exit with a non-zero status.
evidence_produced_by_run: A file was created at a path specified in the attacker-controlled name field, outside the DAG store directory.
The created file contained the unique proof marker EVOHUNT-PATH-TRAVERSAL-PROOF, confirming the content was attacker-controlled.
The file location was verified to be outside the configured DAG directory, confirming the path traversal.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
